en.extract:
The Somali short-toed lark (Alaudala somalica) is a small passerine bird of the lark family found in eastern and north-eastern Africa.
The habitat of the Somali short-toed lark is subtropical or tropical dry lowland grassland.


Taxonomy
The Somali short-toed lark was formally described in 1895 by the English ornithologist Richard Bowdler Sharpe based on a specimen collected on the Haud plateau of Somalia by the American explorer Arthur Donaldson Smith. Sharpe coined the binomial name Alaudala somalica. The Somali short-toed lark was formerly sometimes treated as a subspecies of the Mediterranean short-toed lark (Alaudala rufescens). The Athi short-toed lark was formerly treated as a subspecies.
Four subspecies are recognised:

A. s. perconfusa (White, CMN, 1960) – northwest Somalia
A. s. somalica Sharpe, 1895 – east Ethiopia and north Somalia
A. s. megaensis (Benson, 1946) – south Ethiopia to central Kenya
A. s. athensis Sharpe, 1900 - southern Kenya and northern Tanzania


References

The '''Somali short-toed lark''' ('''''Alaudala somalica''''') is a small [[passerine]] [[bird]] of the [[lark]] family found in eastern and north-eastern Africa.

The habitat of the Somali short-toed lark is subtropical or tropical dry lowland [[grassland]].

==Taxonomy==
The Somali short-toed lark was [[species description|formally described]] in 1895 by the English ornithologist [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe]] based on a specimen collected on the [[Haud]] plateau of Somalia by the American explorer [[Arthur Donaldson Smith]]. Sharpe coined the [[binomial name]] ''Alaudala somalica''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Sharpe | first=Richard Bowdler | author-link=Richard Bowdler Sharpe | date=1895 | title=On a collection of birds made by Dr. A. Donaldson Smith during his recent expedition in Western Somaliland | journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London | pages=457-520 [472] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/30983467 }}</ref> The Somali short-toed lark was formerly sometimes treated as a subspecies of the [[Mediterranean short-toed lark]] (''Alaudala rufescens'').<ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Greenway | editor2-first=James C. Jr | year=1960 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=9 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=51 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14480726 }}</ref> The [[Athi short-toed lark]] was formerly treated as a subspecies.

Four subspecies are recognised:<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=February 2025 | title=Nicators, Bearded Reedling, larks | work=IOC World Bird List Version 15.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/larks/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=10 March 2025 }}</ref>

* ''A. s. perconfusa'' (White, CMN, 1960) – northwest Somalia
* ''A. s. somalica'' [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1895 – east Ethiopia and north Somalia
* ''A. s. megaensis'' ([[Constantine Walter Benson|Benson]], 1946) – south Ethiopia to central Kenya
* ''[[Athi short-toed lark|A. s. athensis]]'' [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1900 - southern Kenya and northern Tanzania

zh.extract:
棕短趾百灵（学名：Calandrella somalica），是百灵科短趾百灵属的一种，是游猎迁徙的候鸟，分布于索马里、肯尼亚、坦桑尼亚和埃塞俄比亚。全球活动范围约为298,000平方千米。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
棕短趾百灵的栖息地为亚热带或热带的（低地）干草原。


参考文献
Calandrella somalica. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27]. （原始内容存档于2016-03-04）.
